item.title: How the pandemic changed work
item.description: Also this week: why more of us need to take a chance on ‘out there’ ideas and innovations

item.title: Five-year UK mortgage rates now below two-year rates in wake of Iran war
item.description: Lenders withdraw more than 1,500 products so far this month

item.title: Germany’s Uniper warns EU methane rules will hit Europe’s energy supplies
item.description: Gas importer says restrictions threaten ‘new layer of challenge’ to continent’s energy security

item.title: ECB warns Hungary’s seizure of Ukraine cash risks euro credibility
item.description: Christine Lagarde raises concerns as Budapest withholds part of cash and gold bound for Ukraine

item.title: KKR and GIP among bidders for Drahi’s multibillion French fibre network
item.description: Bids for XpFibre have come in between €6bn and €8bn as Patrick Drahi pursues asset sales to ease debt burden

item.title: Trump taps Silicon Valley billionaires for policy advice
item.description: Appointments to advisory body deepen ties between tech industry and White House

item.title: Crispin Odey denies trying to ‘manipulate into silence’ victim of sexual assault
item.description: Cross-examination of financier continues in his case against UK regulator

item.title: UK says Chinese wind turbine maker poses national security threat
item.description: Ming Yang’s plans to build Scottish factory thwarted

item.title: Brussels plans carbon price brake as Iran war squeezes industry
item.description: Reforms to EU emissions trading system come after pressure from some member states such as Austria and Italy

item.title: PDD’s magic money machine keeps turning
item.description: Yet the Temu-owner continues to hoard every cent

item.title: FT Wealth: March
item.description: In this edition: Charles Spencer asks what it’s like to live in one of Sir John Vanbrugh’s masterpieces; Clare Maurice examines the tax, visa and estate-planning pitfalls US citizens should weigh before relocating to the UK; Bryce Elder argues that One Hyde Park’s forbidding exclusivity is not a flaw but the very essence of its appeal.

item.title: Military briefing: how a US assault on Kharg Island could unfold
item.description: Seizing Iran’s oil export hub by sea or air would choke regime but risks dragging American troops into open-ended conflict

item.title: Accountancy regulator eases supervision of UK audit firms
item.description: FRC says new approach is a response to a ‘changed world’ after improvements in quality

item.title: Apple rolls out UK age checks for iPhone users
item.description: Move follows pressure from government on smartphone makers to do more to protect children online

item.title: Reliance on imported oil squeezes Japan’s bathhouses
item.description: Middle East conflict is hurting the world’s fifth-largest economy, which sources 90% of its crude from the region

item.title: The ever-changing tune of BlackRock’s Larry Fink
item.description: Chief executive’s annual letter is a study in Trump-era politically correct marketing

item.title: Honda and Sony scrap luxury EVs in latest electric rollback
item.description: Decision casts doubt over future of joint venture combining both Japanese companies’ strengths

item.title: London house prices fall for sixth consecutive month
item.description: Prices in capital diverge from UK growth as buyers look beyond the M25

item.title: This is nuts, when’s the crash — AI cow collar edition
item.description: A heifty funding round

item.title: Merck seals $5.7bn biotech deal to boost cancer drug pipeline
item.description: Terns Pharma stock has jumped since promising clinical data on its bone and blood cancer treatment last year

item.title: War empties Dubai’s malls and rattles struggling luxury industry
item.description: Footfall and sales drop sharply in emirate’s shopping centres as companies battle supply chain chaos

item.title: Italian gunmaker Beretta pushes to raise its stake in Sturm Ruger
item.description: Rifle-maker has dismissed pressure campaign as an ‘ongoing creeping takeover’ as stand-off escalates

item.title: Hedge fund Caxton extends losses to $1.3bn as Iran war rocks markets
item.description: London-based firm has become high-profile victim of market upheaval sparked by Middle East conflict

item.title: African economies brace for fuel shortages
item.description: Some countries across the continent are imposing fines on people hoarding petrol, or subsidising fuel prices

item.title: FirstFT: Markets rally on hopes for a breakthrough in the Middle East
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Democrat wins Mar-a-Lago state seat and blue-chip companies speed up debt-raising plans

item.title: Reeves is right to resist universal help on energy bills
item.description: UK’s fiscal constraints leave government reliant on blunter, supplier-side support

item.title: Pop Mart shares tumble over reliance on Labubu
item.description: Chinese maker of global hit doll is world’s most valuable toy company

item.title: Que Sora, Sora
item.description: RIP Sora. OpenAI video generation platform, 2024-26. We hardly knew ye.

item.title: What Americans moving to the UK need to know about tax
item.description: To make sure you avoid the traps, time your arrival and restructure your trusts

item.title: What’s moving long-term interest rates?
item.description: Plus, US bank regulations
